ogTitle: Marion Bay vs Marks Landing: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Marion Bay and Marks Landing.
heading: Marion Bay vs Marks Landing — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Marion Bay, South Australia and Marks Landing, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Marion Bay has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Marks Landing (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Marion Bay or Marks Landing safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Marion Bay has a flood risk score of 23/100, while Marks Landing scores 25/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Marion Bay scores 46/100 for bushfire risk. Marks Landing scores 56/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Marion Bay or Marks Landing?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Marion Bay has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.48% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
